Five kinds of glock. It's all the same Most of it's all made in one of three places There's three main factories that make all the gloss so when you go to the department store and you walk around those cosmetic counters Though there's five kinds of lipstick. There's five kinds of glock. That's all there is and it's levels of quality. It's just like paint There's five levels of paint That's all there is. That's it the rest of it's all window dressing marketing and One ingredient that doesn't matter at it last that's that whole business this one has Reddlin in it and this one has volcanic ash from India and this one has cheap sperm in it and this one has Well, by the way, it's a big thing in the skincare business There's an entire joke there I could but I won't Oh Yeah, I know but No, no, no, no, no No, there's this I can just picture the heart attack, you know So so Here's what they're doing with all the boosters and they're changing the boosters now so it it's squirt it spray Right, it's another powder you add same with dishwasher stuff by the way You've seen the dish what they do with dishwasher stuff back so they changed the core item But now they also have this whiz bang thing you can add right So tides latest deal are booster pods There's a name they're tied something I forget what they're calling them and they like get out the toughest states So you still use a bucket of tide and then you throw in one of these pods to guess what's in the pod Todd That's what's in the pod. It's tied I mean, it's laughable, but they're changing this like every six weeks So there's always a new and improve which by the way that in and of itself is a stupid statement It's either new or it's improved It's not new and improved It's one or the other But new and improved works better It sounds somehow like more it isn't It doesn't a multi-level I own a piece of an MLM company that I inherited because the company couldn't pay my bills Which by the way is a shitty reason to wind up with part of a company If it can't pay you and they're gonna give you equity instead my advice would be just eat your losses but so We so I doubled the sales overnight of our aerosol shoe Cleaner, you know foam you spray on your shoes out of a can and you clean your shoes so I added spray on the shoe protective coating Same shit both cans just make two different labels and by the way the shoes do look better If you really clean them and polish them up once and then you do it again, which is what this made you do You did the whole thing twice. Oh, yeah, they look people who come in said man, that's really works They look a lot better now that we're doing that. I'm thinking yeah, you know If you go out and wash your wax your car get it all done as best you can and then wash it wax it again It will look better. You'll catch a spot. You didn't catch before it'll shine a little brighter Right same stuff both kids then I pulled the same trick with bug spray So you use a bug spray and then out of this other can you spray this shit that delays the bugs coming back same stuff both But so people People are on this sort of treadmill, you know of wanting it to be new as they get bored with the old and When they are bored with someone else's old It's actually pretty easy to move them To your new that's why in info marketing in every niche Even no matter how crowded it may be the new kid on the block for some period of time does real well Right because they're just Fatigue with the old Hijack 36 This is a cool one the cartoon really tells the story It is about comparative status Or positional status Nobody operates in a vacuum So in reality In most improvement environments The sanest thing to do The healthiest thing to do mentally and emotionally is to better your own best Is to compete with yourself? Right doesn't matter what anybody else is doing the sanest thing is I'm 80 pounds overweight get myself down to 70 get myself down to 60 Etc. All right, I'm making I'm closing 20% Get myself to 25% get myself to 30 but this is not how people function People are constantly comparing themselves to each other in whatever community They are in That's why by the way when you take incentives away So you take positional status away from any group of performers Everybody's performance goes down It's actually the problem with egalitarian socialism Distribute everything even Everybody stops trying everybody Right because there is no difference. So the company that owns Joe's crab shack Which with all their restaurants together? They're the biggest Middle-class restaurant chain in America After six months they have abandoned their no tipping program So they raised everybody's pay Base pay and they eliminated tipping and it lasted six months Variety of things happened Customers don't like it and here's why the customers don't like it because they went from getting good better and best service to getting shitty service So the customers don't like it Customers want to reward and customers want to punish. That's what customers want to do worse Sales of every ancillary item went down Appetizers desserts feature of the day Flat Because there's no board in the back room with people's names on it and Who sold the most pie and who sold the second most pie and why would anybody try and sell pie? They build the check build the tip Everybody gets it and the good ones all left They want to go someplace where their performance is Is openly rewarded Right if I get you to buy a $200 bottle of wine and add it to your check tonight my tips going up Probably disproportionately it won't just be the 20% You'll like the fact that I helped you pick out a really good bottle of wine and you may tip me 30% Right, so this whole experiment is over Now a bunch of other idiots are doing it And the government would like everybody to do it, but the performance falls apart because there's no positional Thing going on. There's no positional status Same thing with salespeople. So this is tried. I don't know how many times Saturn tried it in the car business Right by the way, they're gone in case you haven't noticed There's still Saturn's on the road, but try and get parts So Saturn tried no commissions Little salary our car salespeople Then they will work in the best interest of the customer and they will not be under pressure To earn commissions as soon as you take the board down in the back room Bob Smith is number one Harry's number two Charlie's number three as soon as you don't do the quarterly steak and beans dinner as soon as you stop handing out the plaques Guess what happens? You know, it's only cars Because cars all sales in an organization occur pyramidally just like everything occurs Pyramidally and if there's no positional status, everybody goes on coast All things in suburbia were sold based on keeping up with the Joneses As soon as you sold one swimming pool in a community you could sell five First one was the hardest the next four were easy laydowns They're over there buying them while this one's being put in We were the first people when I was a kid in my rich dad phase We were the first we were the first people to have one of them big-ass redwood above ground pools Had to rip out half the side yard kill some trees move shit put in this big pool right by the way, which Briefly you are now running the local YMCA for the neighborhood. That's what that's That's what you're doing, right? Because every kid is now at your pool. Of course in those days. You didn't there's no lifeguard business. They drowned. They drowned Right, I mean, you know Most of our neighbors were Catholic they can replace them. They're on the rhythm method anyway um Today of course you your lawyers got to be there your insurance agents got to be there But as soon as we put this thing in This guy's buying a pool this guy's getting a pool this guy's getting a pool the pool guys are doing great Soon as somebody in a neighborhood got a new car Color TV You guys remember getting your first cut we were the first ones neighborhood with them huge be a moth Consumes the whole wall console thing with the record player TV About three months took about three months everybody in the neighborhood had Because nobody wants to be down the totem pole It works everywhere So positional status or comparative status is really really powerful and when you can physically do it and Physically show it It's in like in the coaching business if it's sold at seminar We use some version of red room blue room You know everybody that's in the higher level program is dressed So they're all in jackets, so they're all at hats, so they're all you know got epaulets or something They all get to go. This is the diamond lounge when it's done, right at a gk ic event When it's done, right there's a glass wall So everybody can see in there, but they can't get in there unless they are diamond It always upgrades people to die Take as you're selling coaching Everybody who's in Stands up they all get taken out of the room and they get taken over to another room MLM when this was done by the really smart people like the Turner people Everybody's at the big rally right the prospects get left all of the people who are already in get lifted up Lifted up and Taking over into the next room and in those Situations you wanted the holiday in accordion door Because you won't actually wanted the noise from the other room to overpower The noise of the room the prospects were left behind left behind in Because what you did in that room is you brought everybody up on stage and handed out bonus checks Everybody yelled and applauded Okay, everybody this is a group everybody's getting a fifty thousand dollar check come on up, you know, and that's what was going on Well, everybody on the other side is like they don't need to hear shit anymore. They just how do I get? Over there, right? So that's what you do in a physical environment, right? And you use it positively or you use it negatively So the examples I gave you in here are not a physical environment, but so which of these headlines is More powerful take the first two A month sixty for those of you that got lost Lose at least ten pounds and two inches in twenty days summer's almost here Or lose more pounds and inches than any of your friends in the next twenty days summer's almost here Now what I've just said should make it obvious So how many would vote for the first one? Good for you So in an actual split test the second is a winner by three point something to one margin Nothing else changed Third one's a little dicey Which of these three is your winner How many want to vote for number one Okay, how many want to vote for number two? Look around you learn more looking around than you do look at a me All right, how many want to vote for number three? All right more for three than two and obviously none for one, right? You're all right It's virtually a dead heat. It's virtually a tie between two and three However one Way behind way way way behind so positional status and The thing to know about it is you can almost always find a way Has a marketer to give yourself positional status So Almost inexplicably who has more Super Bowl rings Peyton or Eli Eli who's a better quarterback? Of course even a Giants fan has to admit. I mean please there's no comparison Right nor is there in the stats? So Peyton's got any number of bragging stats you want Most number of passing yards most number of this most number of that what he don't have is most number of rings So if he wants positional status against all other quarterbacks He's got it a bunch of other quarterbacks Have positional status the other way In fact up until this win Steve Berg had more rings Than Peyton man if you don't know Google Hertz For 30 years. We're number one Amos we're number two, but we try hard Positional stats Walmart low prices every day the supermarket across the street more bogos than any other store So we can figure out a Positional status place for almost anybody and anything That makes them sound to their marketplace has the winner This whole argument that Bernie was making up until yesterday This whole argument has been about trying to claim positional status that he's the better candidate than Hillary He's losing in every statistical category But he somehow managed to make So his positional argument was what? one candidate he's not losing 18 to 30 So his whole argument has been the future of America Well the elections in November, but the future of America I should be the candidate because this is where we're all headed together Right He's right Technically So you can find a way to create a comparative status argument for just about anybody or anything And then understand that almost everybody is moved by comparative status They aren't looking in the mirror for Self-improvement they are looking around them at everybody else for improvement compared to them See it's not the way in So Weight Watchers is almost everybody in the industry admits Cleveland Clinic says weight Watchers is the most effective Diet plan organized diet of any of them it works better than any of them Weight Watchers is busily fixing that Well they're on their fourth generation of private equity owned You know goofuses who don't understand the business The reason is the way in That's the reason It's the way in They come to a weekly meeting they stand on a scale And Everybody knows what everybody else did So everybody is trying to lose one more pound than that fat bitch Mary who's in the group that nobody right I mean that's what's going on Remove that by the way and it ain't gonna work any better than anything else Do it online in privacy? Worthless MLM tried the same thing they tried to eliminate the meetings. We'll do it all online. Oh, no good What drives the behavior is the 12 people in the Tuesday night living room meeting and who did what? Positional stats that's that's what works hijacked 37 very much tied to it is is an ego is is an ego hook or an ego punch I Don't really need to spend time on it. You'll get it, but it's there for the list so 38 is We don't see things as they are we see them as we are So This is like 50% of my entire premise of selling to the afloat Because the higher up the afloat's ladder you go The less price matters We can buy anything we want And we tend to buy what reflects our self image Poor people often tend to buy what reflects their aspirational Self-image the more money you have the more you tend to buy what reflects your self image And tie it to comparative status All right, so Don't tell him I said this Well, you can actually because he knew it was going to happen Right, so I shouldn't let I shouldn't tell you but it's too late Burleson finally get here. Is he in the room? There you are so When Jay did the ten grand yesterday He said you should be able to use this to get Burleson to do more Burleson's a 20 So Here's what that reflects understand on both their parts it reflects their own self image of who they are Plus it reflects positional staff Right so Now there's no doors I'm safe I Had this thing once I'm on stage like this and there's kitchen behind doors on either end and two guys come out of that door One I'm chasing the other one with a knife I'm serious and and they run right in front of me and they go out that door and Then it's like the cuckoo clock deal with the three stooges right second later. They come out here again the third time The the there they've changed positions The guy with a knife is being chased by the guy without a knife. I'm thinking how dumb are you right? And I mean I'm selling so I don't stop doing what I'm all but they're running in front of me like a cuckoo Makes me flashback so So we often buy and we can be made to buy right by not by what it is but by who we are or Who we like to think of ourselves as being? So if you go to your buy now and buy later our whole discussion this morning during Q&A one way that you move a procrastinating buyer to buying now is Not by demonizing the procrastinator But by flattering him that he is a decisive individual right, so We we buy by who we are and buy in many cases who we like to think of ourselves as being and So I said yesterday, you know it started post World War two and for a long time they owned it until they lost it The Cadillac was a symbol of a rival in America You had made it you were at the top of the Economic and career mountain in your suburban community Cadillac in the driveway nobody parked them in the garages by the way Was it because they were too big it's just they didn't want anybody to miss see of that side of the ditch in their driver So this thing was symbolic of who I am Right I go buy a Cadillac and people bought them because they had arrived or they bought them because they were close And that's that they wanted that to represent what they wanted it to be so with affluent generally What most affluent people will most easily be made to buy Is the thing that is most expensive most exclusive and most symbolic? Though those are your three levers with them So I like having affluent clients by the way it's easier Than having non affluent clients. I spent 20 years lifting up unaffluent clients. I Rather have an affluent one to start with So if you pay attention to Positioning and language and all of that that I do for me I Do several things a I make it very clear that I'm like the most expensive guy doing what I do Mostly we use slightly more elegant language the highest paid Direct response copyright on earth what I'm really saying is I'm the most expensive Copywriter on earth and the affluent client That's the guy one He's the most expensive for a reason Plus I'm going to go brag about Get my stuff done by the most expensive guy on the planet And in whatever little club I'm in appears. I'm going to stick it up everybody else's butt Because I got the most expensive guy and that's exactly what goes on Maybe not exactly enunciated that way to themselves consciously, but that's exactly what's going on Second hard to get to hard to get exclusivity Second thing on the list I just gave which is not in your manual by the way so So most expensive most exclusive I drive both of those points really hard So people go tell their Just wrote about this bill Murray you guys all know who bill Murray is right? So bill Murray is notoriously elusive He doesn't have a cell phone He doesn't answer his phone like ever He's got a flip phone that his grandkids and kids can use and that's it He'll go three months without checking his voice mail He doesn't have an agent Never has had an agent doesn't have a manager. So like if you decide you want bill Murray for a movie It's not like you call William Morris and the conversation starts because first of all somebody's got to find him And so people have these how hard it was to get bill Murray stories We had to we flew a helicopter We flew a plane with a bill call us ban Over all five of his houses for a week. This is a true story until bill finally called us We went all we knew he was in hollywood working We went to all his favorite restaurants and bars and left notes for him hoping he'd come in one of them This way these these are the bill more bill Murray stories, right? well The reason people are telling these stories is Because they like bragging about having got bill Murray That's the deal So, you know the Dan Kennedy stories you can't believe it makes us go to Cleveland. I gotta stay in the Quinta I got a you know Guy or so he didn't have to stay in the La Quinta got a private plane so he can fly in the same damn morning He doesn't have to stay in the La Quinta. This is you know, I mean we had to stay there It doesn't have a restaurant and in the winter to get breakfast We got to walk through six feet of snow over to the Bob Evans You know he's in his basement and if the dog comes down we're interrupted and they got this story, right of the travail You know of what they've gone through in order to It works it also happened, you know, it's convenient that it happens to be what I want to do but But it works so this How we think of ourselves this guy thinks of himself as determined He thinks of himself as once I make a decision This is the guy who to this morning my client is a guy who if he goes to the mall to get shoes he gets shoes That's that's my guy Okay, so once he's decided he's gonna get Kennedy he now moves heaven and earth To get Kennedy and that because that's how he sees himself right people that buy Super-duper expensive kitchen remodeling from a famous named designer.
